questions.question: Why does multi-agent debate break the saturation that self-consistency hits?
options: Debate uses bigger models

Self-consistency samples are i.i.d. from one model so errors correlate; debating agents read and revise against each other's reasoning, decorrelating samples — converging where voting was confidently wrong
Debate runs more samples per second
Self-consistency is incompatible with reasoning tasks
questions.explanation: Doubling i.i.d. samples stops helping because the model averages into its own correlated errors. Debate makes round r+1's context richer than round r's — agents must justify or update when exposed to disagreement.

questions.question: What is the Du et al. (2023) debate algorithm?
options: Two models argue until one concedes
N agents answer independently, then for R rounds each sees the others' previous answers and updates; majority vote at the end
A judge model scores all candidate answers
One agent role-plays both sides
questions.explanation: The ICML 2024 paper's loop beat chain-of-thought and Self-Reflection on MMLU, GSM8K, MATH, biographies, and factuality tasks — Minsky's society-of-mind premise turned into a concrete, measurable procedure.

questions.question: What did the ablations show about agent count vs round count?
options: Only agent count matters
Agent count alone helps but plateaus; rounds alone (self-reflection) barely help; the big jumps need BOTH — multi-round exchange between multiple agents drives the gain
Only round count matters
Neither matters beyond 2
questions.explanation: The two knobs contribute independently and compound. One-shot voting of N agents is weaker than the same N agents exchanging reasoning across rounds — the exchange is the mechanism, not the headcount.

questions.question: What is the trade-off of heterogeneous debate (different base models per agent)?
options: It is strictly better in all cases
Different model families decorrelate errors and reduce monoculture collapse — but a weak model in the debate can drag the consensus toward its wrong answer
It is impossible to implement across providers
It doubles the round count automatically
questions.explanation: Llama + Claude + GPT debating avoids shared family errors, but "Should we be going MAD?" documents the downside: consensus mechanisms give weak participants real influence over the outcome.

questions.question: What is a sycophancy cascade and its mitigation?
options: Agents complimenting users excessively; ban praise words
All agents defer to whichever sounds most confident, collapsing the debate to the loudest voice — mitigated by prompting adversarial roles that must argue the counter-position
Agents agreeing with the most recent speaker; randomize order
A billing failure when too many agents run
questions.explanation: Confidence-following defeats the purpose of debate. The other failure modes: topic drift over rounds (re-inject the question each round) and compute blowup — N×R growing-context calls can cost 10x a single CoT pass.
